boss.lore: Another knight. Different insignia. The corruption is newer — you can tell by the way it still twitches toward memory.
boss.name: The Second Knight

phases.desc: The corruption surges outward, consuming spirit-energy. Overwhelming, and closed to the arcane.
phases.immune_hint: The corruption swallows the spirit-light whole — your focus unravels before it lands. The blade may find what spells cannot.
phases.name: Fracture Surge

phases.desc: The armour knits itself closed from inside. Impenetrable to blades. Slow, but each strike still lands.
phases.immune_hint: The armour has sealed inward — your blade skids across without finding purchase. Something other than steel must break through.
phases.name: Hollow Resolve

ALL_DUNGEONS.locked_lore: Something moved in the sealed chambers beneath the Caelmora plateau. Three survey teams were sent by the Order of the Pale Lantern to investigate. Two returned. Neither filed a complete report.
ALL_DUNGEONS.lore: Beneath the Caelmora plateau, in chambers the Order of the Pale Lantern sealed and forgot, something that was once human still walks the stone corridors. The Hollow Knight was the first. There may be others.
ALL_DUNGEONS.name: The Hollow Sanctum
ALL_DUNGEONS.subtitle: Where corrupted knights still kneel

boss.lore: Not hostile. Curious. But curiosity, in something this old and this alien, looks a great deal like aggression.
boss.name: Echo of the Architect

phases.desc: Testing your parameters. Measuring what you are.
phases.name: Query Protocol

phases.desc: Your existence doesn't fit its models. It panics — but deflects physical strikes through pure calculation.
phases.immune_hint: Logic constructs redirect physical contact before it registers — the Echo calculates around your blade. Shaping-will cuts where steel cannot.
phases.name: Error State

phases.desc: Retreat into pure calculation. Impenetrable to arcane. Still strikes with mechanical precision.
phases.immune_hint: Your spell dissolves into its equations — the Echo consumes spirit-energy as fuel. Old-fashioned force is all it cannot account for.
phases.name: Final Recursion

ALL_DUNGEONS.locked_lore: The lower levels of the Architect Spire have been restricted for as long as the Architects' Children have maintained it. The restriction predates any record in the Ashford archive. What warranted it is not discussed.
ALL_DUNGEONS.lore: The Architect Spire beneath Ashford extends further down than any survey has mapped. In the deepest chamber, something assembled itself from the Spire's logic and the Fracture's hunger. It calls itself an Echo. It is not wrong.
ALL_DUNGEONS.name: The Spire Depths
ALL_DUNGEONS.req_display: Attack 65  •  Meditation 50  •  Spiritweaving 45  •  Q: Echo of Architect
ALL_DUNGEONS.subtitle: The Architects' last calculation

boss.lore: It has no name. It was an experiment that outlived its researchers. The Accord studied what the Architects did to people who failed the Keystone Protocol. This is what they found.
boss.name: The Sealed Accord

phases.desc: Cataloguing. Sorting. Treating the fight as data collection.
phases.name: Archive State

phases.desc: The catalogue breaks. Pure Fracture energy tears apart arcane focus. Only steel endures this.
phases.immune_hint: The Fracture tears your concentration apart — spells unravel mid-cast. Only raw force can meet this storm.
phases.name: Fracture Breach

phases.desc: Attempting self-repair. Fracture energy channels into precise deflection. Arcane strikes bypass the redirect.
phases.immune_hint: It channels the Fracture as precise deflection — your blade meets only redirected force. Shaping will cut deeper than steel here.
phases.name: Containment Protocol

phases.desc: No calculation left. Only force. Hit it with everything.
phases.name: Terminal Phase

ALL_DUNGEONS.locked_lore: The Ashen Accord's central vault has been sealed since before living memory. Whatever they were keeping inside, they decided it was safer locked away than studied further. The Accord does not make that kind of decision lightly.
ALL_DUNGEONS.lore: The Ashen Accord's central vault was sealed for a reason. Not to keep people out. To keep whatever they'd been studying in. Harla knows where the key is. She's not sure you should use it.
ALL_DUNGEONS.name: The Accord's Vault
ALL_DUNGEONS.req_display: Attack 75  •  Meditation 65  •  Shard Gleaning 60  •  Q: Accord Infiltration
ALL_DUNGEONS.subtitle: What the Accord was protecting

boss.lore: Not hostile. Not conscious. A guardian protocol running for centuries in an empty room, waiting for exactly one visitor. It recognises you. It's going to make you prove you've earned the right to be here.
boss.name: The Keystone Entity

phases.desc: Testing all parameters simultaneously. This is an evaluation — not yet a fight.
phases.name: Protocol Awakening

phases.desc: Your spells collapse before they land. The entity channels Fracture frequency directly — arcane force feeds it. Blades only.
phases.immune_hint: The entity draws on the same source as your spells — the Fracture devours its own. Steel must do what spirit cannot.
phases.name: Fracture Resonance

phases.desc: Pure mathematical defence. Physical contact redirected without impact. Arcane force breaks the calculation.
phases.immune_hint: Every physical strike is redirected into the chamber walls — calculated deflection learned from centuries of observation. Shaping-will cuts where steel cannot.
phases.name: Architect's Calculus

phases.desc: The protocol is tearing itself apart. Overwhelming force with no calculation behind it. Hit hard. Don't stop.
phases.name: Keystone Collapse

phases.desc: Everything remaining, all at once. The entity has discarded strategy. It simply will not stop. Neither can you.
phases.name: The Final Variable

pages:
The entity dissolves.

Not dramatically — it simply stops. The light holding its form redistributes into the chamber walls. The room quiets. After centuries of waiting, it has finished its task.

The Architects were thorough. Whatever they designed this for, they built it to last.

The chamber responds.

Patterns trace themselves in Fracture-light across the stone — not random, not decorative. Language. Architect notation, older than any text in Aetheria's libraries. You recognise it. You shouldn't be able to, but you do.

The Keystone Protocol has been executed. The circuit is complete. The notation reads: acknowledged.

You think about what Elowen said, what the Masked Contact said, what Harla warned you about before you opened the Vault. They were all right about pieces of it. None of them saw the whole design.

The Fracture wasn't a wound. It was a door held open by intention — the Architects left it ajar for exactly this. For someone who could walk both sides of it. For the circuit to close itself through a person, not a machine.

That person was always going to be you. The only question was when.

The hum is gone.

The background resonance you stopped noticing because it never stopped — the deep Fracture-frequency that sat under every quiet moment since Caelmora — has been absent for several minutes before you realise it.

The world is just a world. No pull. No sense of something watching from the deep structure of things. Whatever the Architects sealed into this Protocol has been spent. Completed. Released.

Aetheria is breathing out for the first time in a century.

The path back through the chamber is unchanged. The same stone, the same silence. But the Fracture-marks on the walls are dark now — not threatening, not dormant. Finished.

Elowen will want a debrief. The factions will have questions you can't fully answer. The world will keep moving — councils forming, old alliances fraying, new ones written in the margins of the old ones. You'll be part of that.

But here, in this chamber, with the Protocol complete and the circuit closed:

It's done. You're the last person who will ever need to come here.

That's enough.
ending.title: The Keystone Completes

ALL_DUNGEONS.locked_lore: There are places in Aetheria that remain sealed until the right conditions are met — not by mechanism, but by design. The Architects were precise about this. The path to such a place cannot be found until the circuit that leads there is complete.
ALL_DUNGEONS.lore: Beneath everything — beneath the Accord's vault, beneath the Spire's logic, beneath the Fracture itself — there is a room. The Architects sealed it when they left. It has been waiting for the circuit to complete. You completed it. Now it has opened.
ALL_DUNGEONS.name: The Keystone Chamber
ALL_DUNGEONS.req_display: Attack 90  •  Meditation 85  •  Fracture Arts 80  •  Q: The Shattering
ALL_DUNGEONS.subtitle: Where the Architects left their final instruction

ALL_FACTIONS.lore: Scholars, engineers, and obsessives who dedicate their lives to deciphering the original Architects' legacy. Elder Ashwin's quiet authority holds them together. They have no army — only knowledge, which turns out to be enough.
ALL_FACTIONS.name: Architects' Children
ALL_FACTIONS.tagline: They built the world. We read what they left behind.

ALL_FACTIONS.lore: A secret assembly of Fracture-crossed who have been operating in Aetheria's shadows for decades. Their membership is never publicly confirmed. They know things they have no obvious way to know. Their goals are their own.

ALL_FACTIONS.tagline: The world ends in whispers, not speeches.

ALL_FACTIONS.lore: The elected trade council that governs commerce across Ashford's market wards. Guildmaster Fenn has kept the markets running through two Fracture surges and a siege. They are neither heroic nor ideological — they are reliable, which in Aetheria passes for remarkable.
ALL_FACTIONS.name: Ashford Commons Council
ALL_FACTIONS.tagline: Fair prices. Steady hands. Lasting trade.

adventurer.20: The Adventurer's Hall in Ashford posts contracts that few bother to read. You've read them all.
adventurer.40: Your reputation precedes you across the Mire Road. Highwaymen have begun routing around you.
adventurer.60: The Hidden Court has a name for people who operate at this level without choosing a faction: 'convenient.'
adventurer.80: The Ashford Commons Council keeps your name in a file marked 'asset.' You haven't decided if that's flattering.
adventurer.99: Your name appears in enough reports across enough factions that the Shard Wardens have created a dedicated folder.

attack.20: Accuracy is memory. The body learns what the mind once decided.
attack.40: The Ashford fighting schools teach this level in three years. You compressed it.
attack.60: Experienced fighters read an opponent in the first exchange. You've begun reading them before it starts.
attack.80: The Fracture-touched variants are faster, stranger, less predictable. So are you.
attack.99: Every strike is a decision made before the thought. That is mastery — and, the old fighters say, how the Architects designed combat into Aetheria.

cooking.20: In Aetheria, food is survival first and comfort second. You've mastered the first.
cooking.40: A well-cooked meal in Ashford's rougher quarters is the fastest way to be trusted. You've noticed.
cooking.60: You've started adding things the recipes don't list — instinct the traders call 'ghost flavor.'
cooking.80: The Mire Road camps pay double for your rations. Word travels faster than caravans.
cooking.99: What you prepare now sustains more than hunger. It sustains the will to keep going. In Aetheria, that is not a small thing.

crafting.20: Aetheria's materials are stranger than they look. So is what you do with them.
crafting.40: The Ashford market has started to recognize your work before it's labeled.
crafting.60: Craft at this level means understanding what something wants to become, not just what you need it to be.
crafting.80: The crossed artisans — those who remember other worlds — say your hands work like someone who did this before. Perhaps they're right.
crafting.99: What you make outlasts you. In Aetheria, that is the closest thing to permanence anyone has found.

defence.20: The first blow you don't take is better than the best heal you'll ever eat.
defence.40: Caelmora's knights spend a decade reaching this proficiency. You have the record.
defence.60: You've begun to move before the strike lands — not prediction, something slower and deeper.
defence.80: The Hollow Knights of Caelmora were legends before corruption took them. You exceed their documented peak.
defence.99: The best defence is not a wall. It is understanding the attack well enough that walls become unnecessary.

fishing.20: The rivers near Ashford move slow and cold. What moves beneath them moves slower.
fishing.40: Some catches feel less like luck and more like permission. You've stopped arguing with the distinction.
fishing.60: The spirit-touched fish began appearing in your nets. You didn't ask them to.
fishing.80: Tideward's waters are supposedly impassable for anyone below your level. Supposedly.
fishing.99: You've fished the same water long enough to know its moods. It has started to know yours back.

hp.20: Aetheria punishes the fragile. You are no longer fragile.
hp.40: You've taken enough to know how much more you can take. The number keeps rising.
hp.60: Other fighters notice the moment you stop flinching. Enemies notice it first.
hp.80: The Fracture-touched don't distinguish between tough opponents and impossible ones. You are in their second category.
hp.99: You have survived everything Aetheria has placed in your path. It has run out of arguments.

magic.20: The Architect Spire beneath Ashford still hums. Those with enough Fracture Arts can feel it through stone.
magic.40: Fracture Arts in Aetheria are not learned. They are remembered — something your Earth-self stored without knowing.
magic.60: The Ashen Accord recruits specifically for this level of ability. You've declined that invitation by existing outside their reach.
magic.80: Oraewyn's floating islands were raised by mages at this tier. Three centuries ago. The islands haven't come down.
magic.99: You've reached the level the Architects used as a threshold. Beyond this, their records stop — not for lack of room, but lack of language.

meditation.20: Your mind has learned to go quiet. In Aetheria, that is rarer than ore.
meditation.40: The fragments are not dreams. You've accepted that now. The question is whose memories they are.
meditation.60: You've sat still long enough that Aetheria has begun to sit still with you.
meditation.80: The Order of the Pale Lantern offers Meditation rites at this level. You've already passed where their rites end.
meditation.99: You have gone deep enough to touch the place where Aetheria and memory meet. Whatever is down there knows your face.

mining.20: The ore veins of Caelmora's highlands run deeper than any pickaxe before yours.
mining.40: You've learned to read the rock — where Architect-laced stone has folded into something almost alive.
mining.60: The deeper seams hum faintly. The old surveyors called it resonance. You know better now.
mining.80: The Ashlands await. Drakenhollow's forge clans have a name for someone who mines like you.
mining.99: You do not extract ore. You translate it — raw Aetheria into what it was always becoming.

shadow_arts.20: Not everyone in Aetheria operates in daylight. You've learned why.
shadow_arts.40: The Greyfen exile scholars call it 'peripheral truth' — things that exist only when you stop looking directly at them.
shadow_arts.60: The Ashen Accord's shadow network has noticed your work. They've chosen not to interfere. That is either respect or a trap.
shadow_arts.80: What you practice now has a different name in Greyfen. They use it with reverence.
shadow_arts.99: Shadow is not absence. It is the Architects' first medium — the space between decisions where Aetheria was drafted.

smithing.20: The forge demands patience from everyone. From you, it has begun to demand less.
smithing.40: The Drakenhollow clans have a phrase: sek vorrath — 'the iron remembers.' You're starting to understand it.
smithing.60: What you shape now lasts. The Caelmora armorers noticed first. They haven't stopped watching since.
smithing.80: Forge-masters in Drakenhollow earn their titles after thirty years. You are ahead of schedule.
smithing.99: The metal does not resist you. That is the mark. Everything else was practice.

spirit_harvesting.20: The resonance nodes don't appear for everyone. That they appear for you is not an accident.
spirit_harvesting.40: Shard Dust is what remains when Architect code loses its container. You collect the echoes.
spirit_harvesting.60: The nodes pulse in patterns. You haven't decoded them yet. The word 'yet' is doing a lot of work.
spirit_harvesting.80: You're harvesting what the Fracture left behind. It does not object. You find that more unsettling than resistance would be.
spirit_harvesting.99: You are Fracture-attuned. The resonance nodes light before you arrive. The Architects would recognize what you've become.

spiritweaving.20: The foci you weave hold charge longer than the merchants' stock. Nobody's told you why yet.
spiritweaving.40: Spirit doesn't flow — it chooses. You have learned to be worth choosing.
spiritweaving.60: The Spiritweaving masters of Oraewyn, if you ever reach their floating city, will not be surprised to see you.
spiritweaving.80: What you craft now carries a faint resonance signature. Not crafted. Authored.
spiritweaving.99: The Architects wove reality from spirit. You've learned the first vocabulary of a language they spoke fluently.

strength.20: Aetheria's gravity is not quite Earth's. Your body adapted faster than most.
strength.40: The weight you carry now would have stopped you at the gates of Caelmora.
strength.60: Strength in Aetheria is not purely physical. The spirit-touched know this. Enemies feel it.
strength.80: The mountains between Caelmora and Greyfen are called the Ironbacks. You understand the name differently now.
strength.99: The Architects built Aetheria to be survivable. They did not build it to be easy. You have made it easy.

woodcutting.20: The forests near Caelmora are young by Aetheria's count. The ones further east remember the Architects.
woodcutting.40: Some trees step aside for your axe. That is not metaphor.
woodcutting.60: You've started to hear the pause before a tree falls — a silence that feels like held breath.
woodcutting.80: Whisperwood's ancient groves are said to hold memory in their rings. You're starting to believe it.
woodcutting.99: Every log is a year of the world condensed. You've cut more history than most scholars have read.

changes: Fixed: The Worldroot screen opened as a garbled stack — recipe cards collapsed on top of each other and were unreadable. Each recipe card, its ingredient root tiles, and the Forge button now lay out cleanly in sequence. No recipes, costs, or rewards changed.
RELEASES.title: Worldroot Layout Fix: Recipe Cards Now Readable

changes: New: Worldroot — a new menu screen for fusing item clusters into powerful relics. Each recipe is laid out as a tree: the fused relic at the top, its ingredient items rooted below with a live count of how many you own vs. need. When you have everything, a Forge button consumes the ingredients and produces the relic with a celebration. Includes three new legendary relics: Verdant Worldcharm (Mining, Woodcutting, and Fishing bonuses), Arcweave Confluence (Fracture Arts, Spiritweaving, and Meditation bonuses), and Bulwark Heartstone (HP and Defence bonuses). The per-item Upgrade button in inventory is gone — fusion now lives in Worldroot.
New: Compendium — a 100%-completion tracker panel that aggregates every collectible and progression category into one place: items, titles, factions, quests, dungeons, and more.

New: Rewarded ad boosts are now available — watch a short ad for Auto-Meditate, Fracture Surge, or a +4h offline cap extension. Owners of the Warden's Mark can activate all three with a single tap, no ad required.
Improved: The Titles screen now lists every title in the game, including ones you haven't earned yet — greyed out with a short note on how to unlock each, so you can see what's worth chasing.

Improved: When several skills level up at once, you now get a single combined celebration instead of a queue of separate popups. A skill that jumped multiple levels shows the full range (e.g. "Mining 8 → 11").
Improved: Boost buttons now explain when an ad genuinely can't load — the message reads "Ad cannot be loaded — unlock with Warden's Mark" instead of showing the upsell with no reason given.
Changed: The step-by-step mining tutorial has been removed. Starter gathering tools — pickaxe, axe, and rod — are now free to claim directly from the Store.
Fixed: Auto-Meditate could lose part of its XP window when the game processed offline progress at the same time. The full session is now always credited regardless of what else ran on resume.
Fixed: Two Compendium counting issues corrected — boss dungeon loot tables were showing 0/0, and relics forged through Worldroot now register toward the item collection count.
RELEASES.title: Worldroot, Compendium, and Free Starter Gear

changes: Fixed: Switching to another app and coming back could silently lose progress — Shadow Arts steals and all artisan jobs (smithing, cooking, spiritweaving, crafting) did not reconcile the time you were away. An artisan craft running while the app was backgrounded was effectively paused until the app was fully closed and reopened. Returning to the app now credits every system — gathering, combat, Shadow Arts, and Artisan — for the full time you were away, up to the offline cap, whether the app was minimised or closed.
RELEASES.title: Background Progress Fix: Shadow Arts and Artisan Now Caught Up on Return

changes: Fixed: Hardcore characters with no auto-eat could die from full HP despite actively eating — the combat engine processed multiple monster hits in a single pass, leaving no window to eat between them. Low-max-HP characters (e.g. 5 HP) were especially exposed. Live combat now processes exactly one tick per pass when auto-eat is off, so every hit is followed by a screen update and a chance to eat. AFK and unattended deaths are unchanged — that's still the expected hardcore risk.

Fixed: The "Hit X–Y" range shown on the enemy card in combat was computed differently from the actual damage engine, so it could understate the monster's real maximum hit and show a minimum the game never enforced. The displayed range now reads from the same source as the damage roll — what you see is exactly what the monster can do.
RELEASES.title: Hardcore Combat Tick Fix and Hit Range Accuracy

changes: Fixed: The manual Eat button in combat could get stuck disabled after you ate back to full HP. On your next hit, it stayed greyed out even though you were below full HP — the only way to un-stick it was killing the current monster or switching targets. For hardcore characters with no auto-eat, this left you unable to heal mid-fight. The button now re-evaluates every tick, so it becomes available the moment you drop below full HP and have food.

Fixed: Hardcore characters with no boss dungeon first clears saw a greyed "Eat <10%" as the auto-eat dropdown's current selection — making it look like auto-eat was active when it wasn't. Both Combat and Shadow Arts now show "Auto-Eat: OFF" until a threshold is unlocked.
Fixed: The hardcore death screen ("Your hero has fallen.") ran off the right edge on narrow phones and was clipped. The text is now centred and wraps within the screen.
RELEASES.title: Hardcore Eat Fix

changes: New: Hardcore characters can now eat on demand — boss dungeon gating locks auto-eat, but left you with no way to heal in combat or Shadow Arts. A manual Eat button now appears in the combat action row while engaged, and beside the food selector in Shadow Arts. It consumes one serving of your selected food immediately, regardless of threshold or gating. Standard mode is unchanged.
RELEASES.title: Hardcore Manual Eat

changes: Fixed: If a Fracture Surge expired while a smithing, cooking, spiritweaving, or crafting job was running, every subsequent cycle silently kept the surge's halved duration — giving 2× artisan speed for the rest of the session and crediting the full offline window at 2× as well. Cycle timing now re-snapshots on every cycle, so the speed boost ends exactly when the surge does, live and offline.

Fixed: Chronicler catalog title purchases (Vault Runner, Dungeon Legend) were silently lost — shards were deducted, the title never persisted, and the button became purchasable again. Purchases now stick.
New: The exit menu (Android back button) now has a Character Select button. Tapping it saves your character locally and to the cloud immediately, then returns to the character select screen.

Fixed: Leaving a boss dungeon mid-run via the back button or a tab switch left the run in a broken state — offline combat simulation stayed disabled for the session, the dungeon damage bonus kept applying in the overworld, and dungeon music never handed back to the menu track. All three are cleaned up. The run itself is still forfeit.
Fixed: Meditation's level 60 and level 99 milestone XP bonuses (+5% each) never actually applied, even though the wiki listed them. They now apply correctly, live and offline.
Improved: The active-skill bonus line now shows the XP bonus actually being applied, including any milestone bonuses.

Fixed: Cloud sync could, in rare mid-session re-sign-in cases, restore or tombstone-delete the character you were actively playing, losing progress from another device. The actively-played character is now always authoritative and re-asserts itself to the cloud.
Fixed: A slow cloud response arriving after a sync timeout could be applied to the wrong character slot. Responses are now matched to the slot they were requested for.
Fixed: Gold stolen via Shadow Arts while offline now counts toward wealth milestones and achievements, matching active play. Gold from sales that overflows the gold cap no longer counts toward them.
Changed: The Warden's Mark now follows your Google Play ownership — a refunded purchase is revoked on the next successful Play check. Re-purchasing restores it immediately.
Improved: Saves are leaner — removed redundant per-save integrity bookkeeping that had no effect on gameplay. The rolling backup can no longer be overwritten by a corrupted main save.
Fixed: A Fracture Surge expiring during a brief app suspension (under 30 seconds) now credits the surged seconds correctly when you return.
RELEASES.title: Artisan Surge Timing, Cloud Safety, and Dungeon Exit Fixes

changes: Fixed: Fracture Surge's 2× speed boost was applied twice to gathering and Shadow Arts during offline simulation — if a surge was still running when you returned, those skills progressed at 4× speed instead of 2×. Offline hauls are now correct.

Changed: The offline simulation now splits your away time at the exact moment a Fracture Surge expires, for every skill that progresses offline. Away 3 hours with 1 hour of surge left? You get 2× speed for that first hour and normal speed for the remaining two — gathering, combat, Shadow Arts, and artisan skills all follow the same rule. Previously gathering and Shadow Arts credited 2× for the full capped window regardless of when the surge ended, while combat credited none of the boost if the surge had already expired before you returned.
Fixed: A Fracture Surge started during the current session was invisible to the offline simulation — backgrounding and resuming without a full restart meant that surge was ignored entirely.

New: Buff expiry notifications — Fracture Surge faded and Auto-Meditation ended — now fire for every character on the device, not just the one you were last playing. When more than one character has an active buff, the notification names the character so you know who it applies to.

Fixed: Notification timers now reset whenever a buff timer changes — cancelling Auto-Meditation or re-watching a Fracture Surge ad mid-session can no longer leave a stale notification that fires at the old expiry time.
Fixed: Backgrounding the app from the character select screen no longer schedules a spurious Gains Capped notification when no character is actively progressing.
Improved: The launch splash, loading screen, and character select background now all use an identical full-screen cover fit — no more mismatched sizes or letterboxing as the app starts up.
RELEASES.title: Offline Surge Fixes, Accurate Splits, and Multi-Character Notifications

changes: New: Hardcore — auto-eat is now earned through boss dungeon first clears rather than granted by default. One clear unlocks the <10/20/30% thresholds, two clears unlock <40/50%, three unlock <60/70%, and all four unlock <80/90/100%. With no clears, auto-eat is fully locked. Locked thresholds show in the dropdowns alongside what's required to unlock them. Standard characters are unaffected.

Changed: Hardcore combat and Shadow Arts no longer auto-stop when your food runs out — the fight continues and you can die, including while offline. Standard mode keeps the auto-stop behavior and now shows a toast explaining why it stopped.
New: Hardcore character creation now displays the full ruleset before you confirm — no surprises after the choice is made.
New: Hardcore characters have a dedicated Menu entry showing the full ruleset, live boss dungeon first-clear progress, and a per-dungeon checklist of which auto-eat thresholds are unlocked.

Fixed: A fallen hardcore character now blocks every activity — gathering, crafting, meditation, dungeons, and combat — behind a full-screen death resolution prompt. Previously it was possible to keep playing without ever confronting the choice to roll over to Standard or delete.
Fixed: Dying in Shadow Arts as a hardcore character no longer shows a Respawn button with incorrect instructions.
New: The offline report now shows a death warning if you fell in combat or while pickpocketing during the offline simulation — applies to all modes.

Fixed: Retiring a fallen hardcore character now also removes its cloud save. The cloud-save conflict picker can no longer restore a pre-death copy of a fallen hardcore character. Hardcore deaths now upload to the cloud immediately instead of waiting out the sync debounce.

Changed: Adventurer contracts now roll subjects near your actual level in the relevant skill — from 30 levels below up to 3 above. A safeguard prevents the kill pool from going empty when high-level regions are still locked. No more level-4 fishing tasks at Fishing 50.
New: When combat or Shadow Arts auto-stops because your selected food ran out (standard mode), a toast now explains why — previously it stopped silently.

Fixed: The offline simulation screen no longer appears while you are actively playing. It was triggered by brief focus blips (dropdowns, notification shade); it now requires a real 30+ second absence.
Fixed: Starting combat or Shadow Arts while below your auto-eat threshold now heals you up instantly before the first enemy hit can land.
Fixed: The +4h offline cap ad boost now applies when you background and resume the app within the same session — previously it only took effect after a full app restart.

Improved: The enemy card in combat now shows only the stats that matter — melee enemies hide the Fracture Arts level, and caster enemies (Spire Wraith, Grave Hexer, Briar Mystic, Ridge Banshee, Vault Pyromancer, Umbra Magus) hide Atk.

Fixed: Offline combat loot is now listed in the offline report — every item with quantities. The loot was always being awarded; the report just never showed it. Shadow Arts stolen items are now itemized individually instead of a bare count.
Fixed: If your inventory is full during offline combat or Shadow Arts and a new item type has to be discarded, the offline report now lists exactly which items were lost and how many.
RELEASES.title: Hardcore Overhaul, Offline Loot, and Contract Fixes

changes: Fixed: The notification permission prompt never appeared on Android 13+ devices, affecting all players since notifications launched in v0.9.0. On first launch of 0.9.3 you will be asked once; if you previously toggled notifications off in Settings, the toggle now correctly re-requests permission when you turn it back on.
RELEASES.title: Notification Permission Fix

changes: Fixed: Gold rewards from Boss Dungeon Chronicler encounters now save correctly and count toward earning milestones - previously the gold appeared in your wallet but was not recorded, so milestones missed those amounts.

Fixed: Fracture Surge's doubled steal pace no longer drops mid-session when Shadow Arts levels up while you are offline - players with Surge active during a Shadow Arts level-up were receiving fewer steals than they should have.
Improved: The app now does less redundant work on every combat tick, equipment change, and offline resume - most players will notice smoother performance during long sessions.
RELEASES.title: Chronicler Saves, Surge Fix, and Performance

changes: New: Notifications — the app now requests permission on first launch and will not prompt again if you decline.
New: Offline cap notifications now fire at the correct time for your account, including any cap extensions you have active — the old hardcoded 4-hour trigger is gone.
New: A notification fires when your active Fracture Surge is about to expire.
New: A notification fires when your Auto-Meditate session is about to expire.
New: Fracture Surge and Auto-Meditate notifications use separate channels, so you can mute one without silencing the other.
New: Inventory has a Sort row between the search bar and the tabs — sort by Rarity, Worth (quantity × sell price, highest first), or A–Z.
Changed: Inventory tabs are now listed in alphabetical order by display name; the All tab stays first.
Fixed: Swipe gestures no longer fight each other — horizontal swipes across the tab bar and vertical scrolls within a tab now each complete without the other stealing the gesture mid-motion.
Fixed: Auto-meditate sessions are no longer lost when meditation was the only active system — sessions queued while you were away now restore correctly on resume.
Fixed: Returning to the app quickly after a gathering offline simulation no longer grants a second batch of rewards on the next resume.
Fixed: The offline report now shows the full time you were away for gathering-only sessions, not just the capped portion.
Changed: Mining XP reduced by 25% across all nodes to bring it closer to the pacing of other gathering skills.
Improved: Boot splash now fills the full screen on all device sizes.
RELEASES.title: Notifications, Inventory Sort, and Offline Fixes

changes: New: Each adventurer contract now has a Go button that jumps you directly to the right place — gathering opens the skill panel scrolled to the exact resource node, crafting opens the recipe on the correct tab, pickpocket and combat pre-select the target in the dropdowns, and dungeon clear opens the boss dungeon screen with a contract badge on the matching dungeon.
Fixed: Meditation cinematic captions no longer show a large dark box when the caption is only one line — the background now shrinks to fit the text.
RELEASES.title: Go Button and Cinematic Polish

changes: Fixed: Gathering skill panels (Fishing, Mining, Woodcutting, and others) now show a contract badge next to the specific node targeted by an active contract, so you can see at a glance exactly which fish, ore, or log to pursue.
Fixed: Switching combat presets mid-dungeon now correctly preserves your HP as a fraction of your maximum rather than clamping to the old absolute value — full health stays full when you swap gear.
Improved: Cloud saves that are less than 60 seconds newer than your local save are now kept automatically, avoiding unnecessary 'which save?' prompts on quick restarts.
RELEASES.title: Gathering Contracts, Cloud Save, and Dungeon Fixes

changes: New: Inventory has a new Upgradeable tab that gathers the gear pieces you can fuse into a stronger relic (for example the Brute's, Sentinel's, and Warrior's Rings that combine into the Warden's Ascendant Band). Raw crafting materials are kept out of it so the tab stays focused on what's actually upgradeable.

New: The Ashford Commons Council faction can finally earn reputation — it's granted across the Ashford trade arc and the Charter questline. Characters who already finished those quests receive the reputation automatically on load.
New: Boss dungeons now warn you when you're near death with a low-HP danger glow, and play a death animation if you fall.
New: Victory and defeat now have dedicated sound stingers in boss dungeons.
Changed: 'Magic' is now called Fracture Arts everywhere, to match the world's lore.
Fixed: Switching combat presets mid-dungeon now correctly updates your max HP to match the new preset's gear. Previously your HP stayed locked to whichever preset you started the run on.
Fixed: Equipment with skill-XP bonuses no longer lists each bonus twice (the Warden's Ascendant Band, among others, showed its XP bonuses doubled).

Fixed: The offline 'while you were away' report now shows how long you were away even when you spent the whole time on an artisan skill (Smithing, Cooking, Spiritweaving, Crafting) or auto-meditating.
Fixed: Idle progress is no longer lost when you minimise and resume the app.
Improved: The crafting screen now shows a contract badge for every active crafting contract, not just one.
RELEASES.title: The Road to 1.0 — Closed Testing

changes: Fixed: Adventurer contracts could pay far too much XP when you completed a high-level task using a maxed unrelated skill (e.g. a high Shadow Arts pickpocket). Contract XP now scales with your Adventurer level, and gold rewards are rebalanced to scale more fairly with task difficulty.

New: The Pathfinders' Quartermaster now shows what you already have before you spend Seals — gear you own and how many, Streak Insurance charges in hand, whether the Cartographer's Draught is already running, and upgrades you've unlocked.

Changed: Removed duplicate crafted items. A few woodwork pieces (Greyspine Buckler, Emberbark Stave, Deeproot Stave, Vantwood Stave) existed twice; the duplicates are gone — you can still craft the same gear from the Woodwork track.
Improved: Crafted gear names are cleaner — the redundant "Woodwork / Leatherwork / Clothwork" prefix has been dropped since the craft screen already groups by those tabs.
RELEASES.title: Contract Balance and Crafting Cleanup

changes: New: Adventurer contracts are now drawn from across the whole game instead of a fixed list. Almost anything you can do — craft any recipe, gather any resource, slay any monster, pickpocket any mark — can now show up as a contract.
New: Gathering contracts now name a specific resource (e.g. "Mine Thornite Deposit") rather than just the skill.
Improved: Contract XP and gold now scale with the difficulty of the task, and crafting contracts pay extra gold based on how expensive the materials are.
Improved: Contracts stay within reach of your level — you'll never be handed work you can't yet do, and high-level characters mostly see level-appropriate jobs.
RELEASES.title: Anything Can Be a Contract

changes: New: Boss dungeon cards now show the full possible loot table with checkmarks — items tick green as you obtain them across runs.
Improved: Boss dungeon lore text is now hidden until you meet the entry requirements. Locked dungeons show a rumour-tone teaser instead of story details you haven't reached yet.
RELEASES.title: Dungeon Loot Checklist and Lore Polish

changes: New: Contract Streaks — each contract slot builds its own streak as you claim without rerolling. Higher streaks grant bonus Adventurer XP (up to +50%) and a Pathfinder Seal multiplier (up to 3x). Ranks: Bronze (5), Silver (10), Gold (20), Pathfinder (40).
New: Pathfinder Seals — a new currency earned from every claimed contract, scaled by your streak.

New: Pathfinders' Quartermaster — spend Seals on Streak Insurance, Trail Rations (instant XP), the Cartographer's Draught (2x contract progress), adventurer gear, and permanent upgrades (extra contract slot, higher streak cap, more Seals). Stock unlocks by Adventurer level.
New: Streak Insurance — shield one reroll from breaking a slot's streak. You still pay the gold reroll cost.
New: Boss dungeon music overhauled — the Hollow Sanctum, Spire Depths, and Accord Vault each now have a dedicated ambient track.
Changed: Rerolling a contract now resets that slot's streak (the reroll warning shows what you'll lose and offers Insurance if you own it).

Changed: The Trailblazer Compass (Lv 99 mastery relic) now grants +15% to ALL gold earned, game-wide, instead of an adventurer-only reward bonus. Explorer/scout gear still boosts contract rewards as before.
Fixed: Fracture Surge now correctly doubles speed for artisan skills (Smithing, Cooking, Spiritweaving, Crafting).
Improved: Fracture Surge now shows a description of what it does before you watch the ad.
Improved: Story cinematics — 17 visual-only panels now have captions.
Improved: Cloud save conflict picker makes the newer save unmistakable at a glance.
RELEASES.title: Contract Streaks, New Dungeon Music, and Fracture Surge Fixes

changes: New: The UI now fills the whole screen on any device — Galaxy Z Fold (folded and unfolded), Z Flip cover and inner screens, tablets, and small phones. No more black bars or letterboxing.
New: Layout reflows live when you fold/unfold or rotate, switching between compact and wide arrangements automatically.
Improved: On-screen content now respects device safe areas — the tab bar clears the gesture-navigation bar and top content clears camera cutouts and status bars.
Improved: Existing phones are unaffected — standard tall screens render exactly as before.
RELEASES.title: Foldables, Flip Phones, and Every Screen Size

changes: New: Warden's Mark — purchase a permanent item in the Store to unlock all premium ad-gated benefits (Fracture Surge, Auto-Meditate, Extended Cap) without watching ads. Automatically restored across devices.
New: AdMob ads are now live — GDPR/UMP consent is collected on first launch before any ad initialises.
New: Ad-gated boosts (Fracture Surge, Auto-Meditate, Extended Cap) fall back to Warden's Mark activation when ads are unavailable.
Improved: Boot splash now paints immediately with an animated loading indicator before the synchronous scene load.
Improved: HUD status chips use font-safe glyphs instead of emoji — timer (⏱), combat (⚔), and food warning (⚠) now render correctly on all Android devices.
Improved: Status chips wrap to a second row when they overflow the HUD footer.
Improved: Auto-Meditate now locks the manual Begin button while active to prevent double-session.
Fixed: Save encryption hardened against device-ID changes — saves no longer become unreadable after a device reset or OS upgrade.
Fixed: Removed unused Android permissions (USE_EXACT_ALARM, RECEIVE_BOOT_COMPLETED).
Fixed: In-app Privacy Policy link now points to the canonical https://kaldreth.com/privacy.
RELEASES.title: Warden's Mark, Ads Polish, and Stability Fixes

changes: New: Warden's Mark — purchase a permanent item in the Store to unlock all premium ad-gated benefits (Fracture Surge, Auto-Meditate, Extended Cap) without watching ads. Automatically restored across devices.
New: AdMob ads are now live — GDPR/UMP consent is collected on first launch before any ad initialises.

Improved: Auto-Meditate now locks the manual Begin button while active to prevent double-session.
Fixed: Save encryption hardened against device-ID changes — saves no longer become unreadable after a device reset or OS upgrade.
Fixed: Removed unused Android permissions (USE_EXACT_ALARM, RECEIVE_BOOT_COMPLETED).
Fixed: In-app Privacy Policy link now points to the canonical https://kaldreth.com/privacy.
RELEASES.title: Warden's Mark, Ads Polish, and Stability Fixes

changes: New: Fracture Surge — watch an ad on the Skills screen to double the speed of all timed activities (gathering, crafting, shadow arts) for 1 hour. Stack up to 4 ads for 4 hours of 2× speed. XP and affinity XP double as a natural result.
New: Auto-Meditate — watch an ad on the Meditation screen to meditate automatically every cooldown for 4 hours, even while doing other activities.
New: Extended Offline Cap — watch an ad in the Store to add 4 hours to your offline cap for 12 hours. Sleeping players get up to 8 hours of offline progress.
New: Kaldreth Premium — one-time purchase in the Store to permanently skip all ads. Auto-Meditate, Fracture Surge, and Extended Cap all activate with a single tap, forever.
New: Active boosts (Fracture Surge, Extended Cap) show as live countdown chips in the HUD footer.
New: Auto-Meditate offline — sessions that would have fired while you were away are now simulated and shown in the offline report.
New: Keystone Chamber boss dungeon now has its own ambient music track.
Fixed: Cinematic caption overflow — long panels (especially the finale of 'Before') no longer cut off or show two text boxes.
Fixed: Equip comparison dialog now correctly returns the player's choice — a GDScript lambda capture bug meant it always returned 'cancel'.
RELEASES.title: Ads, Fracture Surge, and Premium Upgrade

changes: New: Main background music replaced with a new dark suspense thriller track.
New: The Keystone Chamber boss dungeon now has its own ambient music track.
New: Upgrade system — craft higher-tier items by consuming ingredients from your inventory.
New: Equipment comparison overlay — see stat differences before equipping an item.
New: Ashford Commons Council faction added with new rewards.
New: Lantern Initiate Cape item added.
New: Sigil items now show their source hints in item descriptions.
New: Cinematic panels now play correctly — each tap advances one panel (fixed double-advance on Android touch emulation).
New: Story Memories and Memory Archive now scroll independently on the Meditation panel.
Fixed: Several parse errors that broke the project from compiling (sell_item body duplication, _item_stat_lines missing header, MusicManager const assignment).
Fixed: Burned food items now have a valid item ID and can be discarded.
Fixed: Discard button now appears for zero-value items such as burned food.
Fixed: Skill XP bonus lines now appear correctly on consumable items.
Fixed: Shadow Arts panel header stays pinned while scrolling.
Fixed: Rare equippable shadow arts items no longer trigger a second drop roll.
Fixed: Menu 2-column layout, wiki button, and pickpocket calculation.
Fixed: HP bar colours now reflect health percentage.
Fixed: Notification UX improvements.
Fixed: Boss dungeon header subtitle no longer overlaps the wave label during runs.
Fixed: Continue button shows 'Loading…' while scene transitions.
Fixed: Mastery relic sell prices capped correctly.
RELEASES.title: New BGM, Keystone Music, Upgrade System, and Bug Fixes

changes: New: Meditation now unlocks story cinematics — 7 sequences (34 illustrated frames) that play automatically as you reach meditation milestones, telling Rynen's story before and during the Fracture. Tap to advance each panel.
New: Story Memories gallery on the Meditation panel — every unlocked cinematic can be rewatched at any time.
New: Meditation milestones reached through quest XP also unlock their cinematic at the next meditation session.
New: Skill Tree, Factions, Memory Fragments, and Boss Dungeons panels now show a lore subtitle in their header.

Improved: Offline simulation processes significantly faster — XP level calculations are now 'O(1)' instead of recomputing the full curve each tick. Artisan offline simulation no longer blocks a single frame.
Fixed: Game crashed on launch with 'Invalid access to property clear on Dictionary' — two overlay autoloads were passing built-in container methods as Callables, which GDScript does not support.
Fixed: INTEGER_DIVISION warning in offline report duration formatting.
RELEASES.title: Meditation Cinematics and Offline Improvements

changes: Fixed: Monster HP bar now correctly updates each tick — it was frozen at full, making it look like no damage was being dealt.
Fixed: New characters can now see damage land and XP tick up immediately.
New: Combat XP now awards per hit, scaled by damage dealt — no more waiting for a kill to see progress.
New: All combat styles give 6 XP per damage — single styles award it all to one skill, Balanced splits 2 ATK + 2 STR + 2 DEF. Magic (Fractured Arts) gives 6 XP per damage.
New: Defence XP no longer awarded for taking hits — it now comes entirely from dealing damage.
New: HP XP is awarded on every hit at 2x damage dealt.
New: Overkill damage no longer grants bonus XP — only the HP you actually removed counts.
New: A short respawn delay (~2s) occurs after each kill before the next monster activates.
RELEASES.title: Combat Fixes and XP Rework

changes: New: Hardcore characters who die must choose to delete the character or roll over to Normal — there is no respawn.
New: If you close the game on a fallen Hardcore character, the character select screen now shows FALLEN in red so you know before loading in.
New: Deleting any character now requires typing the character's name to confirm — recovery is not possible after deletion.
Fixed: Hardcore death dialog can no longer stack if you navigate away before choosing.
RELEASES.title: Hardcore Death and Deletion Safety

changes: New: Every skill now has a distinct colour — panels, headers, and labels tint to match.
New: Shadow Arts, Meditation, and Adventurer panels now show tinted headers matching their skill colour.
New: Boot screen picks a random background illustration each launch.
New: Static in-game wiki added.
Balance: All crafted gear stat progressions rebuilt from calibrated per-tier tables — armour and weapons now scale smoothly across tiers.
Balance: Mountain rat difficulty and Cairncast gear stats rebalanced.
Fixed: Several equipment pieces were missing level requirements or showing zero stats — both corrected.
Fixed: Gathering progress bar no longer drifts out of sync with the server.
Fixed: Burned food (unobtainable at max Cook level) no longer appears in the item collection log.
Fixed: Contract rerolls no longer suggest a duplicate contract you already hold.
Fixed: Android back button now always shows the quit-confirmation dialog.
RELEASES.title: Gear Tables and Visual Polish

changes: New: Felling skill now shows a unique tree illustration for each tree type in the node panel.
New: Each tree's log item now has a distinct cut-log icon that matches its tree's appearance.
New: Full art pass — all 331 game icons regenerated with improved lore-accurate images.
New: Strength and Fracture Arts skill icons redesigned — gauntlet for Strength, cracked iron sphere for Fracture Arts.
Fixed: Weekly tasks were resetting unexpectedly on login due to an unstable character creation date anchor. Now uses a fixed global Monday epoch — all characters reset on the same day.
Fixed: Offline simulation now enforces a 4-hour cap. The offline report shows both how long you were away and how much counted.
Fixed: Offline processing screen now warns against closing mid-simulation and blocks the back button.
RELEASES.title: Art Pass, Offline Fixes, and Felling Trees

changes: New: Fourth boss dungeon — The Keystone Chamber. Five brutal phases ending in a story epilogue.
New: Staves and clothwork gear now require Magic level instead of Attack/Defence.
New: Keystone Architect's Seal — a new jewel reward from the Keystone Chamber.
New: Animated character select screen with a panning, breathing background and floating lantern effects.
New: Panel headers now tint to each skill's colour with a subtle grain overlay.
Fixed: Dungeon clear requirements are now properly enforced on boss unlock gates.
RELEASES.title: The Keystone Chamber

changes: New: Item icons now appear in gathering node boxes and artisan recipe rows.
New: Each boss dungeon has a unique phase shift sound — original, reversed, and a glitchy remix.
New: Boss dungeon BGM ducks during phase shift stings.
