ALL_QUESTS.desc: You appeared from nowhere and the air still hums where you stood. The Wandering Scholar wants to know what you remember — and so do you.
ALL_QUESTS.name: Awakening Echo
ALL_QUESTS.npc_dialogue: You… you appeared from nothing. The air still hums where you stood. What do you remember?

ALL_QUESTS.desc: Prove to the Order of the Pale Lantern that you've found your footing in Aetheria.
ALL_QUESTS.name: Lantern Initiate
ALL_QUESTS.npc_dialogue: We see them sometimes — people who fall through. Most don't survive the first week. You're still here. That means something.

ALL_QUESTS.desc: Strange beasts circle the highland resonance nodes. Investigate the source.
ALL_QUESTS.name: Spirit-Touched Beasts
ALL_QUESTS.npc_dialogue: The beasts don't attack unprovoked. They circle the nodes like they're guarding them. You've worked the earth and forest enough — you'll know what doesn't belong.

ALL_QUESTS.desc: Sustained meditation has begun to surface something buried. A warmth. A sound. A word in a language that is almost yours.
ALL_QUESTS.name: The Pale Flame
ALL_QUESTS.npc_dialogue: You're not sleeping — I can tell. You're going somewhere when you close your eyes. Most newcomers never feel that. Don't ignore it.

ALL_QUESTS.desc: A corrupted Lantern Knight has been seen on the plateau. The Order asks you to end it — and keep the insignia.
ALL_QUESTS.name: Fallen Knight
ALL_QUESTS.npc_dialogue: He was a good man. Whatever is wearing his armour is not. Be careful — Fracture-touched combat is unpredictable even for the trained.
ALL_QUESTS.req_display: Attack 30  •  Total Level 150  •  Q: Lantern Initiate

ALL_QUESTS.desc: The fishing and spirit-harvesting guilds are at odds over shared node territory. Someone who knows both trades could broker a peace.
ALL_QUESTS.name: Guild Bonds
ALL_QUESTS.npc_dialogue: They both want the same resonance pools. You look like someone who's actually gotten their hands wet in both. Maybe they'll listen to you.

ALL_QUESTS.desc: A cluster of resonance nodes near Caelmora's eastern ridge has gone dark. Cleanse them before the plateau loses its spirit anchor.
ALL_QUESTS.name: Highland Cleansing
ALL_QUESTS.npc_dialogue: We've been monitoring those nodes for three years. Whatever's happening accelerated after the Hollow Knight appeared. This is connected.
ALL_QUESTS.req_display: Shard Gleaning 25  •  Total Level 200  •  Q: Guild Bonds

ALL_QUESTS.desc: The meditation sessions are becoming something else entirely — not just calm, but contact. Someone is reaching back.
ALL_QUESTS.name: Whispers from the Plateau
ALL_QUESTS.npc_dialogue: I've seen this progression before. Once, decades ago, with someone who later disappeared into the Spire ruins beneath Ashford. I'm not telling you that to frighten you.

ALL_QUESTS.desc: Evidence has emerged that a faction within the Order may have been deliberately allowing Fracture corruption to spread.
ALL_QUESTS.name: Order's Fracture
ALL_QUESTS.npc_dialogue: I don't want to believe it either. Which is why I need someone else to go look. If I find what I think I'll find, I can't be the one who found it.
ALL_QUESTS.req_display: Defence 35  •  Total Level 250  •  Q: Fallen Knight

ALL_QUESTS.desc: Face the corrupted Order champion deep in the plateau caverns.
ALL_QUESTS.name: Hollow Knight
ALL_QUESTS.npc_dialogue: You've earned the right. Whatever is inside that armour — it will tell you something about where you came from.
ALL_QUESTS.req_display: Attack 50  •  Meditation 25  •  Q: Order's Fracture + Whispers
ALL_QUESTS.reward_display: Memory Fragment  •  3 Mastery Points  •  Unlocks Mire Road

ALL_QUESTS.desc: The Mire Road to Ashford is newly passable — but barely. A merchant caravan needs protection through the marsh.
ALL_QUESTS.name: Mire Crossing
ALL_QUESTS.npc_dialogue: Pay's fair. Danger's real. The marsh trolls have been Fracture-touched since last season — stronger, meaner, and they travel in packs now.

ALL_QUESTS.desc: Ashford is louder than Caelmora. And somewhere beneath the chaos, someone has been watching for your arrival.
ALL_QUESTS.name: Whispers in Ashford
ALL_QUESTS.npc_dialogue: You feel it too, don't you? That pull. Like the city is trying to remember something. Come find me when the meditation takes you somewhere you don't recognize.

ALL_QUESTS.desc: The ruined Architect Spire beneath Ashford is humming. Weaving and harvesting together can unlock its deeper chambers.
ALL_QUESTS.name: Spire Resonance
ALL_QUESTS.npc_dialogue: The Spire isn't dead. It's waiting. What it's waiting for is someone who can hear both sides of the resonance at once.
ALL_QUESTS.req_display: Shard Gleaning 35  •  Spiritweaving 30  •  Q: Whispers in Ashford  •  Clear: Hollow Sanctum
ALL_QUESTS.reward_display: Memory Fragment  •  Unlocks Shard Gleaning (Shard Dust)

ALL_QUESTS.desc: The Spire ruins beneath Ashford are infested with Fracture-touched wraiths — and something commanding them.
ALL_QUESTS.name: Fracture-Touched Underbelly
ALL_QUESTS.npc_dialogue: Leth's letter arrived ahead of you. He wants someone he trusts in those ruins. Which means he trusts you.

ALL_QUESTS.desc: Meditation 40 opens a frequency no human should be able to hear. Something waits in the deepest chamber.
ALL_QUESTS.name: Echo of the Architect
ALL_QUESTS.npc_dialogue: Whatever you encounter down there — don't try to understand it. Just listen. The ones who tried to argue with it didn't come back the same.
ALL_QUESTS.req_display: Meditation 40  •  Total Level 500  •  Q: Fracture-Touched Underbelly
ALL_QUESTS.reward_display: Memory Fragment  •  Architect Shard  •  5 Mastery Points

ALL_QUESTS.desc: The Hidden Court — a secret assembly of Fracture-crossed — has called its first full meeting in years. Elowen has arranged your invitation.
ALL_QUESTS.name: Hidden Court Convening
ALL_QUESTS.npc_dialogue: You feel it too — the pull of the old world in your bones. Everyone in that room does. The question is what you do with it.

ALL_QUESTS.desc: Tally records show suspicious trades funding the Ashen Accord. Following the trail requires trading contacts.
ALL_QUESTS.name: Merchant Intrigue
ALL_QUESTS.npc_dialogue: I shouldn't know what I know. But I do. And I need someone who can move money without raising flags.

ALL_QUESTS.desc: Elder Ashwin has devised a ritual that could anchor Rynen's spirit projection more deeply to Aetheria.
ALL_QUESTS.name: Stabilization Ritual
ALL_QUESTS.npc_dialogue: The world no longer feels like a distant dream to you. The next step is letting it feel like home. Close your eyes. I'll do the rest.

ALL_QUESTS.desc: Fracture Arts and spiritweaving together can reach areas of the Spire that physical force cannot. A hidden archive awaits.
ALL_QUESTS.name: Shadows of the Spire
ALL_QUESTS.npc_dialogue: The Architects didn't just build rooms. They built frequencies. Weave and cast at the same time and the walls will open for you like a door recognizing a key.
ALL_QUESTS.req_display: Fracture Arts 45  •  Spiritweaving 40  •  Q: Spire Resonance

ALL_QUESTS.desc: Three factions now look to you. Each has something the others need. Bind them — or watch the Accord pick them apart.
ALL_QUESTS.name: Crossed Alliances
ALL_QUESTS.npc_dialogue: The Shard Wardens need a first recruit. The Architects' Children need a champion. The Hidden Court needs a face they can trust. You're all three.
ALL_QUESTS.req_display: Total Level 700  •  Q: Stabilization Ritual + Intrigue + Shadows

ALL_QUESTS.desc: The Ashen Accord's stronghold locations are now known. Infiltrating one requires every skill and every alliance you've built.
ALL_QUESTS.name: Accord Infiltration
ALL_QUESTS.npc_dialogue: We know where they are. We know what they want. We don't know how far along they are. That's the only thing that still scares me.
ALL_QUESTS.req_display: Attack 60  •  Meditation 55  •  Total Level 750  •  Q: Crossed Alliances  •  Clear: Spire Depths

ALL_QUESTS.desc: Unite the factions under a shared charter. Diplomacy as weapon.
ALL_QUESTS.name: The Federator's Path
ALL_QUESTS.npc_dialogue: You really think you can bring the Accord to the table? Some of them. The ones who are afraid. The ones who joined because they had nowhere else to go.

ALL_QUESTS.desc: Dismantle the Accord from inside through intelligence, manipulation, and carefully placed betrayal.
ALL_QUESTS.name: The Shadow Hand's Path
ALL_QUESTS.npc_dialogue: You don't believe in clean hands. Good. Clean hands don't win wars. They just make the losers feel better about themselves.

ALL_QUESTS.desc: Aetheria needs a single, unambiguous force of authority. You have grown powerful enough to be that force — and know what it costs.
ALL_QUESTS.name: The Sovereign's Path
ALL_QUESTS.npc_dialogue: I've watched this path before. I won't try to stop you. I'll ask only this: remember who you were before the power.

ALL_QUESTS.desc: Your path is chosen. The Crown of Fractures is within reach.
ALL_QUESTS.name: The Branching Crown
ALL_QUESTS.npc_dialogue: Every path leads here. The Accord is cornered. The allies are gathered. What happens next is the story you tell Aetheria about itself.
ALL_QUESTS.req_display: Total Level 800  •  Attack 70  •  Meditation 65  •  Shard Gleaning 60
ALL_QUESTS.reward_display: 8 Mastery Points  •  Crown of Fractures Title

ALL_QUESTS.desc: Meditation 70 is a threshold. Beyond it, the barrier between Rynen's Earth memories and Aetheria's present collapses entirely.
ALL_QUESTS.name: Fracture's Heart
ALL_QUESTS.npc_dialogue: You're close to the full recall. The Architects designed this. You are not discovering a secret. You are completing a circuit.
ALL_QUESTS.req_display: Meditation 70  •  Q: The Branching Crown  •  Clear: Accord Vault

ALL_QUESTS.desc: The Shard Wardens need to be more than a title. Recruit, train, deploy.
ALL_QUESTS.name: Warden's Resolve
ALL_QUESTS.npc_dialogue: I've been making a list. People I trust. People who've been watching you since Caelmora. They're ready. Are you?

ALL_QUESTS.desc: The final assault on the Accord stronghold requires not just combat strength, but crafted armaments.
ALL_QUESTS.name: Stronghold Assault Prep
ALL_QUESTS.npc_dialogue: I've seen the schematics. We have one shot. One. The prep work is everything. Don't cut corners because you're tired of waiting.
ALL_QUESTS.req_display: Attack 75  •  Smithing 60  •  Cooking 50  •  Q: Warden's Resolve

ALL_QUESTS.desc: Every memory fragment, every ally, every skill — converging. The Architects' circuit is nearly complete.
ALL_QUESTS.name: Echoes Converge
ALL_QUESTS.npc_dialogue: I've heard the Architects called it the Keystone Protocol. The Fracture wasn't a catastrophe. It was a test.
ALL_QUESTS.req_display: Total Level 1200  •  Q: Assault Prep + Fracture's Heart

ALL_QUESTS.desc: Complete, collapse, or stabilize the Fracture. This is the world you made.
ALL_QUESTS.name: The Shattering
ALL_QUESTS.npc_dialogue: Whatever you decide — this is the world you made. Own it. Completely.
ALL_QUESTS.req_display: Attack 80  •  Meditation 80  •  Shard Gleaning 75  •  Q: Echoes Converge

ALL_QUESTS.desc: The Shattering left three factions pointing fingers. Elowen wants someone to put chairs in a room — and make everyone sit down.
ALL_QUESTS.name: The First Council
ALL_QUESTS.npc_dialogue: Every faction survived the Shattering. Most of them are pointing fingers. Someone needs to put chairs in a room and make them sit down. That someone is you.
ALL_QUESTS.req_display: Q: The Shattering  •  Q: The Federator's Path

ALL_QUESTS.desc: Commander Leth's faction will not join any council that includes the Hidden Court unless its membership is formally disclosed.
ALL_QUESTS.name: The Lantern's Demand
ALL_QUESTS.npc_dialogue: The Court operates without accountability. I understand their reasons. I'm telling you those reasons don't matter anymore. Aetheria needs daylight. Get me names.

ALL_QUESTS.desc: The Hidden Court will step into the light — but only if Rynen personally guarantees their safety.
ALL_QUESTS.name: The Court's Condition
ALL_QUESTS.npc_dialogue: We've operated in shadows because shadows kept us alive. You want us in the open — then be the thing between us and those who would use our names against us. Personally.

ALL_QUESTS.desc: The question of Accord amnesty goes before a formal council vote. Leth will accept the outcome — but he won't like it.
ALL_QUESTS.name: The Amnesty Vote
ALL_QUESTS.npc_dialogue: I'll accept the outcome. I won't like it. But I'll accept it. That's what the Charter means — that I give up the right to make this call alone.
ALL_QUESTS.req_display: Total Level 1050  •  Q: The Court's Condition

ALL_QUESTS.desc: An Accord hardliner cell rejects the amnesty offer and raids a Caelmora supply route. The deadline is now.
ALL_QUESTS.name: The Splinter Cell
ALL_QUESTS.npc_dialogue: I told you some of them wouldn't take it. The amnesty offer has a deadline now. After that, they're not remnants. They're enemies. Go remind them of the difference.

ALL_QUESTS.desc: Rynen's Earth memories are offered as testimony before the Council — proof the Fracture was designed, not accident.
ALL_QUESTS.name: Memory Testimony
ALL_QUESTS.npc_dialogue: You carry evidence no Aetherian could fabricate. The Architects chose you specifically. If you testify what you remember, every faction will have to reconsider everything.

ALL_QUESTS.desc: Add a formal clause to the Charter protecting Earth-crossed people from exploitation. Someone needs to propose it who doesn't look self-interested.
ALL_QUESTS.name: The Architect Clause
ALL_QUESTS.npc_dialogue: There are others like you. Fewer than ten that we know of. The Charter can protect them — if someone proposes the clause who doesn't look like they're doing it for themselves.

ALL_QUESTS.desc: The Charter of Fractures is ready for signature. Every faction. One document. The people who said this was impossible are watching.
ALL_QUESTS.name: Charter Signing
ALL_QUESTS.npc_dialogue: Every faction. One document. You realize how many people told me this was impossible? More than I'd like to count. They're all in this room right now watching you prove them wrong.
ALL_QUESTS.req_display: Total Level 1300  •  Q: The Architect Clause

ALL_QUESTS.desc: A Fracture node destabilizes near Ashford. Three factions respond under the new Charter's joint protocol — for the first time.
ALL_QUESTS.name: The Charter's First Test
ALL_QUESTS.npc_dialogue: The resonance went sideways near Ashford. Three factions, joint response. It went... actually well. Don't tell anyone I sound surprised. Sister Vayne out.

ALL_QUESTS.desc: Rynen is formally named First Federator of Aetheria. History will have opinions about everything you've done. It can't argue with the document.
ALL_QUESTS.name: Federator's Legacy
ALL_QUESTS.npc_dialogue: History will have opinions about everything you've done. What history can't argue with is that you sat everyone down and made them write it down together. That's yours.

ALL_QUESTS.desc: The Masked Contact sees opportunity in the post-Shattering chaos. The Accord's network is scattered — not destroyed. Gather it before someone else does.
ALL_QUESTS.name: Handler's First Task
ALL_QUESTS.npc_dialogue: Chaos is a door. You know how to walk through doors. The Accord's network isn't destroyed — it's scattered. Let's gather it before someone who doesn't share your priorities does.
ALL_QUESTS.req_display: Q: The Shattering  •  Q: The Shadow Hand's Path

ALL_QUESTS.desc: Shadow Arts are needed to extract financial records from a collapsed Accord safehouse — every transaction, every name.
ALL_QUESTS.name: The Accord's Ledger
ALL_QUESTS.npc_dialogue: Every name, every transaction. If we have the ledger, we determine who was coerced, who was bought, and who was a True believer. Only one of those groups is actually a problem.
ALL_QUESTS.req_display: Shadow Arts 50  •  Q: Handler's First Task

ALL_QUESTS.desc: The ledger reveals Merchant Harla was feeding intelligence to three separate factions simultaneously — a secret she's been carrying alone.
ALL_QUESTS.name: Harla's Confession
ALL_QUESTS.npc_dialogue: You don't look surprised. I was trying to make sure no single faction got enough to start another war. Whether that was noble or self-preservation — I'm still deciding.

ALL_QUESTS.desc: Some exposed Accord members carry skills the new Aetheria needs. Whether you can separate past from utility is the real test.
ALL_QUESTS.name: The Useful Traitors
ALL_QUESTS.npc_dialogue: I don't care about their past. I care about what they can do now. The question is whether you can separate those two things as cleanly as I can. You probably can't. That's fine — I'll do that part.

ALL_QUESTS.desc: Elowen draws a line at what she'll sanction from the Shadow Hand's methods — not a request.
ALL_QUESTS.name: Elowen's Line
ALL_QUESTS.npc_dialogue: I'm not asking you to stop. I'm telling you what I won't defend if it comes out. There's a list. Don't cross what's on it. That's not a request.
ALL_QUESTS.req_display: Total Level 1050  •  Q: The Useful Traitors

ALL_QUESTS.desc: Build a continent-wide intelligence relay using dormant Architect infrastructure. Spiritweaving is the only way to reactivate the nodes.
ALL_QUESTS.name: The Signal Network
ALL_QUESTS.npc_dialogue: The Architects built this for something else entirely. What they built is perfect for what we need. I'm choosing not to think too hard about why that is.

ALL_QUESTS.desc: A planted operative inside the Accord's Ashford remnant cell has gone dark. Either compromised — or they found something too dangerous to transmit.
ALL_QUESTS.name: The Mole's Silence
ALL_QUESTS.npc_dialogue: Either they're compromised, or they found something too dangerous to risk transmitting. Either way — go get them. Come back with both the operative and whatever they found.

ALL_QUESTS.desc: The recovered intelligence reframes the Accord entirely — they weren't trying to control the Fracture. They were trying to reverse it. Send everyone home.
ALL_QUESTS.name: What Was Found
ALL_QUESTS.npc_dialogue: They weren't just trying to control the Fracture. They were trying to reverse it. Send everyone back. Some of them were desperate, not evil. That changes the calculus.

ALL_QUESTS.desc: Formally dissolve the Accord while preserving its useful elements as a new intelligence agency — with rules even the Shadow Hand follows.
ALL_QUESTS.name: Dissolution Architecture
ALL_QUESTS.npc_dialogue: You turned an enemy into a tool. Some people will call that dangerous. They're right. The question is whether you're the right person to hold it. I think you are. I'm not sure if that reassures me.

ALL_QUESTS.desc: Formalize an intelligence compact that operates alongside the open government — no names, no addresses, a set of rules that even the Shadow Hand follows.
ALL_QUESTS.name: The Shadow Charter
ALL_QUESTS.npc_dialogue: No names. No addresses. A set of rules that even we follow. You made me agree to something I'd never have agreed to before you. Don't tell anyone.

ALL_QUESTS.desc: The Shattering left a power vacuum. The first decree under Sovereign authority sets the precedent for everything that follows.
ALL_QUESTS.name: First Proclamation
ALL_QUESTS.npc_dialogue: Every law has a cost — to someone. You know that. The question is whether you count those people before you sign. I'm asking you to count them. All of them.
ALL_QUESTS.req_display: Q: The Shattering  •  Q: The Sovereign's Path

ALL_QUESTS.desc: Commander Leth publicly questions whether Rynen's authority is legitimate — not a rebellion, a question that deserves an answer.
ALL_QUESTS.name: Leth's Challenge
ALL_QUESTS.npc_dialogue: I'm not rebelling. I'm asking. There's a difference, and you should understand that difference better than anyone. What gives you the right? Give me an answer I can defend.

ALL_QUESTS.desc: A buried Architect record reveals a Sovereign Path was attempted once before — and ended in failure. Elder Ashwin wants you to know what you're solving.
ALL_QUESTS.name: The Architect Precedent
ALL_QUESTS.npc_dialogue: The first Sovereign lasted nineteen years before they were undone by the faction system they'd tried to replace. I'm not telling you this to stop you. I'm telling you what you're solving.

ALL_QUESTS.desc: The Hidden Court will recognize Sovereign authority only if Rynen commits to specific, unbreakable protections — not promises, guarantees.
ALL_QUESTS.name: The Court's Terms
ALL_QUESTS.npc_dialogue: They've been burned by authority before. You understand that better than you think. They want guarantees — not promises. Promises are what the Accord gave them.
ALL_QUESTS.req_display: Total Level 1050  •  Q: The Architect Precedent

ALL_QUESTS.desc: Publicly try captured Accord leaders to establish the precedent for Aetherian justice. Sovereignty must be visible — messy and potentially wrong.
ALL_QUESTS.name: The Accord Trial
ALL_QUESTS.npc_dialogue: This is the hardest part of sovereignty — that justice has to be visible. Messy. That it looks like you could be wrong. Because you could be wrong. And that has to be allowed.
ALL_QUESTS.req_display: Total Level 1150  •  Q: The Court's Terms

ALL_QUESTS.desc: Meditation 75 surfaces a specific Earth memory: Rynen in a leadership role, and what that role cost. Both parts matter.
ALL_QUESTS.name: The Iron Memory
ALL_QUESTS.npc_dialogue: You led something. I can see it in the way you stand when decisions are made. You lost something because of it too. Remember both things at once. That's where the actual wisdom lives.

ALL_QUESTS.desc: The moment of choice: govern alone, or establish a council with genuine authority. One path survives you. One only works while you're here.
ALL_QUESTS.name: Council of Equals
ALL_QUESTS.npc_dialogue: This is the moment. Either you're building something that survives you — or something that only works while you're here. Both are choices. Only one of them is what you said you wanted.

ALL_QUESTS.desc: Recover the Accord's highest-chamber artefact — a Fracture-fused crown. What you do with it is the most visible choice you've made.
ALL_QUESTS.name: The Fracture Crown
ALL_QUESTS.npc_dialogue: They kept it as a symbol of control. You can destroy it, wear it, or put it somewhere visible so people never forget what unchecked authority produces. I know what I'd do. I'm asking what you'll do.
ALL_QUESTS.req_display: Attack 80  •  Shard Gleaning 75  •  Q: Council of Equals

ALL_QUESTS.desc: Establish what happens to Aetherian authority if Rynen is ever gone. Institutions that outlast people are the only ones worth building.
ALL_QUESTS.name: Succession Protocol
ALL_QUESTS.npc_dialogue: Institutions outlast people. Good ones, anyway. The worst thing a leader can do is refuse to plan for their own absence. I've seen it happen too many times. Don't be that kind of leader.

ALL_QUESTS.desc: Final acknowledgment of the Sovereign Path — what it cost, what it built, and why the weight was always the point.
ALL_QUESTS.name: Sovereign's Burden
ALL_QUESTS.npc_dialogue: You built it. It's heavier than you thought. I'm telling you that's not a flaw in the building. That's how it's supposed to feel when it matters.

mf_accord_revelation.text: A chamber. Seven seats — only three occupied. A document passed hand to hand, each signature leaving a burn mark on the page. You understand the words but not who you were to be in the room. The fourth seat was yours. You never sat down. The Fracture took you first.
mf_accord_revelation.title: What the Architects Agreed

mf_accords_origin.text: The Ashen Accord wasn't born from politics. It was designed — by someone who understood Architect systems and wanted to control the Keystone completion. They were afraid of what the Protocol would do if the wrong people held the circuit. They might not have been wrong.
mf_accords_origin.title: Where the Accord Came From

mf_aetheria_name.text: In the document — the one with the burning signatures — there's a name for this world. Not Aetheria. Something older. A designation, not a name. Forty-seven characters. You can read seven of them. One of them is your name.
mf_aetheria_name.title: Named

mf_ashford_echo.text: Somewhere in your chest when you first entered Ashford — recognition. Not of the city as it is, but as it was. Before Caelmora, before the Accord, when the Spire was new and the streets were different. You shouldn't know what a city looks like before you were born.
mf_ashford_echo.title: City Beneath City

mf_builders_mark.text: An Architect glyph carved into a wall in the Spire. You've seen it before — not in Aetheria. On a blueprint. In a project file. On something you worked on that you never understood why you were asked to build.
mf_builders_mark.title: Recognized

mf_circuit_board.text: A workshop. Your hands soldering a board that doesn't exist here — copper traces, resistors, a processor you'd spent two months sourcing. You were building something you believed in. The fact that it couldn't follow you through feels like theft.
mf_circuit_board.title: Dead Technology

mf_crown_weight.text: A project meeting where everyone was looking at you. Not for guidance — for permission. At some point you'd become the person whose 'yes' was required, and you hadn't noticed the moment it happened. That's the thing about accumulating authority — it doesn't announce itself.
mf_crown_weight.title: In Charge

mf_earth_decision.text: An offer. Real money, a real job, a real future — or the thing you actually loved, which paid nothing and mattered everything. You chose the thing you loved. Three weeks later, the Fracture opened. You'll never know if you chose right. You're starting to think that's the point.
mf_earth_decision.title: The Choice Before the Fall

mf_earth_work.text: Three years of your life. A system for something you believed in — more equitable, more transparent, better than what existed. You never finished it. The Fracture didn't let you. You wonder sometimes if Aetheria is the test version.
mf_earth_work.title: The Project

mf_elara_last_message.text: A notification you never opened. From Elara. Sent four hours before the Fracture. The preview shows only three words and you can almost read them — but the memory stops there, like a sentence ended by silence. You've stopped trying to force it.
mf_elara_last_message.title: Unread

mf_elara_voice.text: Not a face. A voice — warm and slightly too fast, like she was always one sentence ahead of everyone else. Elara. The name comes before the rest of it. She was the one who told you the door was probably nothing. You both laughed. Then you walked through it.
mf_elara_voice.title: Her Name Was Elara

mf_federator_memory.text: A civic hall on Earth — cheap folding chairs, someone speaking too quietly into a microphone. You voted for something that didn't pass. You stayed and helped anyway. The person next to you said: 'The point isn't the outcome. The point is showing up.' You still don't know if that's wisdom or cope.
mf_federator_memory.title: The Vote

mf_fracture_moment.text: Not a memory. The memory. A notification on your phone: coordinates, a date, a name — yours, but wrong. A door in the sky. You walked through it because curiosity beat fear by half a second. Aetheria snapped shut behind you like a book.
mf_fracture_moment.title: The Fracture Moment

mf_fracture_opened.text: The door in the sky — you have three seconds of clear memory before everything blurs. First second: the edge of it, like a heat shimmer with depth. Second second: your hand reaching out without your permission. Third second: the sound of everything you'd ever known receding, like tide pulling back before a wave.
mf_fracture_opened.title: Three Seconds

mf_hollow_knight.text: A knight kneeling. Not yours — someone else's memory, bleeding through. Armour cracked open like an egg. Inside, nothing but pale light and the smell of burnt circuit boards. You understand suddenly: they were also brought here. They also forgot. The corruption was mercy.
mf_hollow_knight.title: Iron and Light

mf_home.text: You've stopped dreaming about Earth. The dreams are Aetherian now — the plateau ridge, the mire smell, the particular quality of light through spirit-touched leaves. Home isn't a place you left. It's a weight distribution that shifted while you weren't watching.
mf_home.title: Home

mf_keystone.text: Not an accident. Not a catastrophe. A design with seven phases — you're in phase three, maybe four. The Fracture pulled specific people, not random ones. Each of you carries a piece of the circuit. When the circuit completes, the Keystone Protocol executes. No one told you what it does.
mf_keystone.title: The Keystone Protocol

mf_keystone_completed.text: The Keystone Protocol has been executed. The circuit is complete. The Fracture-notation in the chamber walls reads: acknowledged. You are the only person in Aetheria who has seen that word in Architect script. You are not entirely sure what you agreed to.
mf_keystone_completed.title: Acknowledged

mf_pale_flame.text: Warmth before language. Your hands cupped around something small and pale — not a candle, not quite. It pulsed. Like a heartbeat that wasn't yours. Elara's voice from somewhere behind you: "Don't be afraid of what you can hold." You weren't afraid. That was the problem.
mf_pale_flame.title: First Fire

mf_resonance_map.text: A schematic you drew on a whiteboard on Earth — half-remembered, half-invented, for a project that got cancelled. You recognise it now. It's a resonance node map. You mapped Aetheria's energy infrastructure two years before you arrived.
mf_resonance_map.title: Blueprint

mf_seven_seats_truth.text: The signing chamber again — but now you can count. Seven chairs. Three signatures that burned the page. One empty seat was yours. The other three seats held people whose faces blur when you look directly at them. The document passed without you. Something broke when it did.
mf_seven_seats_truth.title: The Seventh Chair

mf_shadow_cost.text: A workplace. A thing you saw that you chose not to report because you didn't think it was your place, and then it became someone else's problem in a much worse way. You still think about that. You probably always will.
mf_shadow_cost.title: What You Didn't Report

mf_tech_echo.text: Scrolling text on a monitor. Green on black. A language you know — knew — in another life. The Architects didn't build Aetheria from stone. They built it from logic. From recursion. Every skill you learn is a subroutine remembering itself.
mf_tech_echo.title: The Code

mf_the_meeting.text: A boardroom. Not the one under the Spire — the other one. Earth. Fluorescent lights. Three people waiting for someone who never showed. You were told to come back later. The door was locked when you returned. The Fracture opened two days after.
mf_the_meeting.title: Seven Chairs, Three People

mf_the_notification.text: A phone screen. Your phone screen. A notification you didn't send to yourself — coordinates, a timestamp set three weeks in the future, and a single word: REMEMBER. The timestamp is the exact moment you stepped through the Fracture.

mf_the_other_three.text: The other occupied chairs. The more you meditate, the more the faces clarify at the edges. Someone who worked with their hands. Someone who moved through institutions the way water moves through cracks. Someone who was very, very afraid. You recognise the way afraid people hold themselves. You've looked like that.
mf_the_other_three.title: The Others
